symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible exertional calf claudication relieved by rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible exertional pain relieved by rest is the hallmark of arterial claudication and the precursor to critical ischaemia.
Vascular claudication is relieved simply by standing still; neurogenic claudication typically needs a change in spinal posture (flexion) to ease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic rest pain in the toes, worst lying flat and relieved by dependency
symptoms.onset: 48 hours, on a background of a year of claudication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest pain relieved by dependency is close to pathognomonic of critical limb ischaemia — perfusion only just meets resting demand with gravitational assistance.
Neuropathic pain is not positionally relieved by limb dependency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevation pallor and dependent rubor (positive Buerger's sign)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional colour change reflects severely reduced arterial inflow; the patient can report the colour change over video even though the graded clinician test is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses with diminished popliteal pulse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent pedal pulses are a core objective sign of critical arterial insufficiency — a clinician-only finding not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cool, pale, shiny skin with loss of hair over the toes (trophic changes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic trophic skin changes indicate long-standing severe ischaemia; the visible appearance is partly patient-observable, the graded temperature assessment is clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed capillary refill (>4 seconds) in the right toes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged capillary refill reflects poor distal perfusion; a clinician-measured examination finding.

investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated < 0.4, consistent with critical limb ischaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of ischaemia is not objectively graded and revascularisation planning is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ABI < 0.4 objectively confirms critical arterial insufficiency; requires in-person measurement, never available in telehealth.
Tobacco use is the dominant modifiable risk factor for peripheral arterial disease and its progression to critical ischaemia.
Absence of guideline antiplatelet therapy is a care gap and a management target rather than a diagnostic feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden onset, numbness or paralysis of the limb (pertinent negative for acute limb ischaemia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The subacute worsening with preserved sensation and movement points away from a complete acute occlusion — but does not eliminate the need to safety-net for it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral swelling, warmth or erythema (pertinent negative for DVT)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cool, pale, pulseless limb is the opposite of the swollen, warm, erythematous DVT picture.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Reproducible claudication, ischaemic rest pain, elevation pallor/dependent rubor, absent distal pulses and an ABI < 0.9 together define critical limb ischaemia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectively grades the severity of arterial insufficiency and confirms critical ischaemia; performed in person, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial duplex ultrasound of the lower limb
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Localises the level and severity of arterial occlusion to plan revascularisation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT or MR angiography of the lower limb
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the arterial anatomy for endovascular or surgical revascularisation once the limb is under vascular-surgery care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting glucose / HbA1c and lipid profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies co-existing diabetes and dyslipidaemia driving atherosclerosis — for ongoing risk-factor management, not the acute priority.
The clinical picture is arterial, not venous thromboembolic; a D-dimer would not change management and risks a distracting false positive.
